steps.title: Recall the parallel lines test with a transversal
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two lines, several conditions prove the lines are parallel:
  • Corresponding angles are equal
  • Co-interior (consecutive interior) angles sum to 180°
  • Alternate interior angles are equal
We know $w = 160°$ at the intersection of line $k$ and line $r$. We need to identify which angle relationship at line $s$ would prove $r \parallel s$.

steps.title: Analyze angle positions and test choice B
steps.content:
At the intersection of $k$ and $r$: angle $w = 160°$ is formed on one side of the transversal.

At the intersection of $k$ and $s$: angle $y$ is in the corresponding position to $w$ (same relative position at the second intersection).

If $y = 160°$, then $w = y$. Since both angles are in corresponding positions and are equal, the corresponding angles theorem guarantees that lines $r$ and $s$ are parallel.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
